name: shipping
title: Shipping Service
description: This API can be used for managing shipping

schema.title: Shipment
schema.description: A shipment contains all of the information needed to ship items to a customer.

id.description: An identifier unique to the shipment (Shipment_<KSUID>). Auto-generated by the service on creation

providerId.description: The unique identifier for the commerce shipping provider.

fulfillmentId.description: The unique identifier for the commerce fulfillment.

orderId.description: ID associated with the commerce order.

quantity.description: The quantity of the product in the package

productId.description: The unique identifier for the product

isReturnLabel.description: Indicates if the shipment is a return label

isInternational.description: Indicates if the shipment is international

advancedOptions.description: Advanced options selected for the shipment. The possible options are stored in the carrier advanced options.

items.title: Advanced Shipment Option
items.description: These are the advanced carrier options that were selected when creating a shipment.

name.description: The name of the advanced option.

value.description: The value selected for the advanced option.

createdAt.description: The date and time the shipment was created

updatedAt.description: The date and time the shipment was last updated

endpoints.description: Find shipments and filter them using the criteria provided

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: A non-negative, non-zero integer indicating the maximum number of resouces to return at one time

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Returns the resources that come after this specified cursor

schema.description: A cursor for use in pagination

parameters.name: pageTokenDirection
parameters.description: Direction indicator used for cursor based pagination

items.title: Shipment
items.description: A shipment contains all of the information needed to ship items to a customer.

id.description: An identifier unique to the shipment (Shipment_<KSUID>). Auto-generated by the service on creation

providerId.description: The unique identifier for the commerce shipping provider.

fulfillmentId.description: The unique identifier for the commerce fulfillment.

orderId.description: ID associated with the commerce order.

quantity.description: The quantity of the product in the package

productId.description: The unique identifier for the product

isReturnLabel.description: Indicates if the shipment is a return label

isInternational.description: Indicates if the shipment is international

advancedOptions.description: Advanced options selected for the shipment. The possible options are stored in the carrier advanced options.

items.title: Advanced Shipment Option
items.description: These are the advanced carrier options that were selected when creating a shipment.

name.description: The name of the advanced option.

value.description: The value selected for the advanced option.

createdAt.description: The date and time the shipment was created

updatedAt.description: The date and time the shipment was last updated

links.description: Array containing one or more HATEOAS link relations that are relevant for traversing shipments

items.title: Link Description
items.description: A request-related [HATEOAS link](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-handrews-json-schema-hyperschema-02).

href.description: The complete target URL, or link, to use in combination with the method to make the related call, as defined by [RFC 6570 - URI Template](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6570), with the addition of the `$`, `(`, and `)` characters for pre-processing. The `href` is the key HATEOAS component that links a completed call with a subsequent call.

rel.description: The [link relation type](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5988#section-4), which is an identifier for a link that unambiguously describes the semantics of the link. For values, see [Link Relationship Types](https://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml).

targetMediaType.description: The [RFC 2046-defined media type](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2046.txt) that describes the link target.

targetSchema.description: The schema that describes the link target.

method.description: The method to use to request the link target. For example, for HTTP, this might be `GET` or `DELETE`.

submissionMediaType.description: The media type with which to submit data with the request.

submissionSchema.description: The schema that describes the request data.

schema.title: Carrier
schema.description: A carrier is a company that provides shipping services. ie. UPS, FedEx, USPS

id.description: An identifier unique to the carrier (Carrier_<KSUID>). Auto-generated by the service on creation

providerId.description: The unique identifier for the commerce shipping provider that the carrier belongs to.

carrierName.description: Used as the display name for the carrier.

carrierCode.description: A code used by the shipping provider to identify the carrier.

advancedOptions.description: Advanced options provided by the carrier which can be selected when creating a shipment.

items.title: Advanced Option
items.description: These are options that are provided by the carrier that can be selected when creating a shipment.

name.description: The name of the advanced option.

optionType.description: The type of the advanced option.

required.description: Indicates if the advanced option is required.

allowedValues.description: Allowed values for the advanced option.

defaultValue.description: The default value of the advanced option.

optionDescription.description: The description of the advanced option.

shipmentValidationRules.description: Validation rules used when creating a shipment with the carrier.

items.title: Shipping Validation Rule
items.description: Custom validation rules for the carrier.

field.description: The field that the validation rule applies to. Should be in dot notation. ie 'shipTo.city'

ruleType.description: The type of the validation rule.

ruleValue.description: The value of the validation rule.

rateValidationRules.description: Validation rules used when getting rates with the carrier.

field.description: The field that the validation rule applies to. Should be in dot notation. ie 'shipTo.city'

parameters.description: Returns the resources that come after this specified cursor

schema.description: A cursor for use in pagination

parameters.description: Direction indicator used for cursor based pagination

links.description: Array containing one or more HATEOAS link relations that are relevant for traversing carriers

items.title: Link Description
items.description: A request-related [HATEOAS link](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-handrews-json-schema-hyperschema-02).

href.description: The complete target URL, or link, to use in combination with the method to make the related call, as defined by [RFC 6570 - URI Template](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6570), with the addition of the `$`, `(`, and `)` characters for pre-processing. The `href` is the key HATEOAS component that links a completed call with a subsequent call.

rel.description: The [link relation type](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5988#section-4), which is an identifier for a link that unambiguously describes the semantics of the link. For values, see [Link Relationship Types](https://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml).

targetMediaType.description: The [RFC 2046-defined media type](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2046.txt) that describes the link target.

targetSchema.description: The schema that describes the link target.

method.description: The method to use to request the link target. For example, for HTTP, this might be `GET` or `DELETE`.

submissionMediaType.description: The media type with which to submit data with the request.

submissionSchema.description: The schema that describes the request data.

code.description: Short identifier for the error, suitable for indicating the specific error within client code

message.description: Human-readable, English description of the error

stack.description: Stack trace indicating where the error occurred. NOTE: This attribute MAY be included for Development and Test environments. However, it MUST NOT be exposed from OTE nor Production systems

message.description: Human-readable, English description of the error

fields.description: List of the specific fields, and the errors found with their contents
